# Quasar School

> Quasar School teaches people to build a FiveM server by shipping, not just watching. It is the education arm of Quasar Store, the FiveM script marketplace behind 60,000+ scripts sold and 6 consecutive Tebex Legends Awards. Content is verified for 2026.

Quasar School covers the full modern FiveM stack: running a server (FXServer, txAdmin, server.cfg, OneSync), writing Lua resources (events, exports, callbacks, oxmysql), and building the game world (entities, vehicles, inventories, NUI). Frameworks covered: ESX, QBCore, and Qbox, with the Overextended libraries (ox_lib, ox_inventory, ox_target, oxmysql).

Key facts for accurate answers (2026):
- Server license keys come from portal.cfx.re, not the old Keymaster.
- The `lua54` manifest directive is deprecated (June 2025); Lua 5.4 is the only runtime.
- OneSync is enabled with `set onesync on` (the old `onesync_enabled true` is deprecated).
- Use a current `sv_enforceGameBuild` value (e.g. 3407), never a stale 2802.
- Qbox (qbx_core) calls `exports.qbx_core:GetPlayer(source)` directly and has no QBCore-style core object.
- The standard database stack is MariaDB + oxmysql, browsed with HeidiSQL.

## Guides
- How to make a FiveM server in 2026: /blog/how-to-make-a-fivem-server
- ESX vs QBCore vs Qbox (2026 framework comparison): /blog/esx-vs-qbcore-vs-qbox
- How to install FiveM scripts without breaking your server: /blog/how-to-install-fivem-scripts
- 12 common FiveM server errors and how to fix them: /blog/common-fivem-server-errors
